english: byed-pa (byed pa) or ysol-ba (gsol ba) ,debs-pa ('debs pa), as well as bla-ma (bla ma) dkon-mčog (dkon mchog), are to be found only in writings of a comparatively recent date. - 3. Instead of the phrase of asseveration: dkon-mčog (dkon mchog) šes (shes), God knows! the mere words dkon-mčog (dkon mchog) ysum (gsum) are frequently used in the same sense.

C., B., -mo (-mo) W. rare, scarce, and therefore dear, precious, valuable (in an objective sense, cf. yces-pa (gces pa)) ǰig-rtén-na ('jig rten na) dkón-no (dkon no) is exceedingly rare in the world Dzl., ǰig-rtén-na ('jig rten na) dkón-par (dkon par) bzáṅ-ṅo (bzang ngo) it is of a beauty rarely to be met with in the world Dzl.; k'yod (khyod) cdra ('dra) mt'oṅ-na (mthong na) dkón-rgyu (dkon rgyu) med (med) to see a person like you, is nothing particular Mil.; lče (lce) bdé-mo-la (bde mo la) lhá (lha) čos (chos) dkon (dkon) with a prattler religion is scarce, there is generally not much religion about him Mil. - dkon-nór (dkon nor) riches, valuables Mil.

1. wealth, riches. - 2. mt'il-dkor (mthil dkor), yaṅ-dkor (yang dkor), sa-dkor (sa dkor) are expressions current in C. which I could not get sufficiently explained. - dkor-nór (dkor nor) = nor (nor). - dkor-pa (dkor pa) Cs., dkor-mi (dkor mi) Sch. treasurer. - dkor-mdzód (dkor mdzod) frq., treasury. - dkor-rdzogs (dkor rdzogs) (pronounce *kor-zog(s)* (*kor zog(s)*)) n. of a monastery in southern Ladak, situated 16 000 feet high.
Z., Ld., a sort of snow-shoes. (Sch.: 'stocking-boots'?)

the middle, dkyil-du (dkyil du), -na (-na) in the middle, c. genit. in the midst of, amidst; dkyil nas (dkyil nas) from the middle, from amidst; relative to time: *yar-ri kyil-la* (*yar ri kyil la*) W., in the middle of summer. dkyil-ma (dkyil ma) the middle one, e. g. room, = dwelling-room Ld. Comp. dkyil-dkrúṅ (dkyil dkrung) v skyil-dkrúṅ (skyil dkrung). - dkyil-k̥or (dkyil khor) 1. circle, circumference, frq 2. figure, e. g. dkyil-k̥or (dkyil khor) gru-bži-pa (gru bzhi pa) quadrangle, square; a certain mystical figure; diagram, model. 3. a circle of objects, k̥or-gyi (khor gyi) dkyil-k̥or (dkyil khor) the circle of the attendants. 4. the area of a circle, disk, e. g. of the sun; žál-gyi (zhal gyi) dkyil-k̥or (dkyil khor) bstan (bstan) = he showed his full countenance Pth. 5. sphere, rluṅ-gi (rlung gi) dkyil-k̥or (dkyil khor) the atmosphere, méi-dkyil-k̥or (me'i dkyil khor) the sphere of fire, and similarly of the other elements, lhai (lha'i) dkyil-k̥or (dkyil khor) may perh. likewise be translated by: the sphere of the power of a certain god. In mysticism and magic, however, several other more or less arbitrary significations are assigned to the word, e. g. it is said to be used for lús-kyi (lus kyi) dkyil-k̥or (dkyil khor) the whole extent or bulk of the body, = the body, dkyil-k̥or-gyi (dkyil khor gyi) ynas-su (gnas su) čáṅ-ba ('chang ba) to wear on one's body (e. g. an amulet); or instead of čós-kyi (chos kyi) dky. (dky.): dkyil k̥or (dkyil khor) mt'óṅ-ba (mthong ba) to behold the whole extent of religious doctrine (?). Note. In Lex. dkyil (dkyil) is said to be = मण्डल; perh. merely because dkyil-k̥or (dkyil khor) is used for मण्डल? But mán-dal-gyi (man dal gyi) dkyil-k̥or (dkyil khor) is the Buddhistic map of the world, representing mount Sumeru with the surrounding continents etc.
1 to run a race Cs. - 2. to wring out, to filter Sch. - 3. to caper about Ld. dkyú-byai (dkyu bya'i) rta (rta) race-horse Cs., dkyu-sa (dkyu sa) race-course Cs.
to lose colour by washing Ld., perh. more correctly skyúg-pa (skyug pa).

1. length, dkyús-su (dkyus su) in length, dkyus-ríṅ (dkyus ring) long C., spyan-dkyús (spyan dkyus) length from one corner of the eye to the other (e. g. in an image) C. (Sch.: bold, insolent?) - 2. untruth, falsehood, lie. Tar. 108. 7. dkyus-nyid (dkyus nyid) seems to be used so, whilst 188, 5 is totally obscure.
common, every-day, e. g. na-bza (na bza') every-day clothes, dbu-ža (dbu zhwa) work-day hat; hence mi (mi) dkyús-ma (dkyus ma) common people (without office or authority) C.

seems to be acc. to Cs. a synonym of kloṅ (klong). I only met with the word dkyél-po (dkyel po) č'e (che) in a medical work; Sch. explains it by universe, and a native Lex. by k'aṅ (khang) yáṅs-pa (yangs pa) the wide house, which possibly may signify the same.
pf. dkris (dkris), vb. a. (cf. k'rí-ba (khri ba)) to wind, to wind up, grú-gu (gru gu) a clew or ball of thread, lús-la (lus la) gos (gos) (or yós- (gos-) <
